title: qa-redteam-2026-06-25-followup: close B2/C3/D3 recipe shortfalls flagged by final review
body:
# Goal

Close the three recipe-shortfalls identified by the final whole-branch
review of QA round 2026-06-25. Each is a documented gap in the
harness — not a defect in jjf itself — that prevented the round
from reaching its declared coverage. Run the three recipes
properly; file any new findings under
`-l qa-redteam-2026-06-25` so they roll up with the round.

# Findings to close

## (1) B2 silent-no-op assertion gap

The `sub2-wronganswer.sh::b2` matrix exercises 40 `(status, verb)`
cells but only asserts on exit code and stderr-regex. A verb that
"succeeds" with exit 0 yet performs no state change on a terminal
issue (e.g. `unblock` on `open`, `open` on `open`) would not be
flagged. The wrong-answer class explicitly includes silent
no-ops.

**Fix:** extend `b2` so after each verb run, also call
`jjf show --json <id>` and assert the post-status matches the
spec's status-machine cell. Re-run the matrix; file any new
finds as `sev:wrong-answer`.

## (2) C3 10MB body silently accepted

`sub3-panic.sh::c3` ran a 10MB body via `-F <bigfile>` and
reported `accepted (no cap, no panic)` as a `negative` verdict.
The spec's exact wording was "no documented cap — capture
behavior"; silent acceptance of an unbounded body IS a
`sev:contract-drift` finding (the contract is ambiguous /
missing).

**Fix:** file a small `sev:contract-drift` ticket titled
roughly "no documented body-length cap; 10MB accepted silently"
asking the spec to either declare a cap or document the
unbounded behavior explicitly. Cite `cli-json.md` for what a
typed-error envelope would look like if rejected.

## (3) D3 ConcurrentWrite.hint never snapshotted

`sub4-contractdrift.sh::d3` uses `cp -R` to clone the scratch
repo to a sibling for the race. Both pushes succeeded in the
implementer's run, so the race never produced divergence and
`ConcurrentWrite.hint` text remained un-snapshotted. The plan
documented a fallback: `git clone "$bare" "$sib"; cd "$sib";
jj git init --git-repo=. --colocate`.

**Fix:** rewrite `d3` (or add a `d3b` variant) using the
fallback recipe. Capture the actual hint text. Check whether it
contains substring-matchable phrases ("another writer landed
first", "concurrent write", "conflict", "stale"); if any do,
file a `sev:contract-drift` ticket so the message either gets
stabilized or split into structured fields.

# References

- Round spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-25-qa-redteam-sweep-design.md`
- Round plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-25-qa-redteam-sweep.md`
- Harness: `experiments/qa-redteam-2026-06-25/`
- Round closing comment on `5a755ec epic:agent-ergonomics`
- Final reviewer's report archived in this session's scratchpad
